hero.subtitle1: 54 composable skills wired into a single
hero.social: Open source · MIT License · Works offline

problem.label: 01 / the problem
problem.title: AI agents are capable but undisciplined.
problem.card1Desc: They ship code that looks correct but breaks in production.
problem.card2Desc: They overwrite each other's work without noticing.
problem.card3Desc: They produce the same patterns regardless of context.
problem.metr: When developers used AI, they took 19% longer. Developers estimated AI saved them 20% of the time. Experts predicted 38% faster.
problem.conclusion: The gap between "capable" and "reliable" is not intelligence. It's process.

pipeline.label: 02 / design review
pipeline.title: From Critique to Delight
pipeline.subtitle: A 9-stage design review pipeline triggered after Verify. Each stage is a composable skill that targets a specific dimension of production-grade output.
pipeline.critique: Two-pass design review with personas and scoring
pipeline.clarify: UX copy rewrite for clarity and tone
pipeline.footnote: Each stage is a composable skill. Use them all, or pick what you need.

references.label: references
references.title: Sources
references.metrTitle: Becker, J., Rush, N., Barnes, E., & Rein, D. (2025).
references.metrPaper: Measuring the Impact of Early-2025 AI on Experienced Open-Source Developer Productivity.
